docs.title: FallbackRabbit
docs.text:
Never let your LLM calls fail.

FallbackRabbit generates, tests, and optimizes intelligent fallback chains for any LLM provider. Define your chain once, simulate outages, and export to your framework of choice.

docs.title: Why FallbackRabbit?
docs.text:
• 🔍 Smart Fallback Chains — Priority-ordered provider chains with automatic failover
  • 🧪 Simulated Testing — Test outage scenarios before they happen in production
  • 📦 Multi-Format Export — LiteLLM, OpenRouter, LangChain, Haystack, and custom configs
  • ⚡ Live Dashboard — Dark-themed web UI for chain management and real-time testing
  • 🔐 Auth & Rate Limiting — Production-ready security out of the box
  • 🔌 Real Provider Calls — Test against actual OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure, and Ollama endpoints

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
pip install git+https://github.com/MelaBuilt-AI/FallbackRabbit.git

# Create a chain config
fallbackrabbit init my-chain.yaml

# Validate it
fallbackrabbit validate my-chain.yaml

# Test with simulated outages
fallbackrabbit test my-chain.yaml

# Start the dashboard
fallbackrabbit serve --port 8000

docs.title: Features at a Glance
docs.text: Feature Description Chain Builder Build priority-ordered provider chains with validation Simulator Inject outages, test failover paths, measure latency Export Output to LiteLLM, OpenRouter, LangChain, Haystack, custom CLI Full-featured command-line interface REST API 15+ endpoints for chain CRUD, testing, and export Dashboard Dark-themed SPA with WebSocket live progress Auth Static API keys, Bearer tokens, query param auth Rate Limiting Token bucket, per-IP + global, burst support Storage In-memory or SQLite persistence WebSocket Real-time test progress broadcasting

docs.title: License
docs.text: MIT — use it however you want.

docs.title: REST API
docs.text: FallbackRabbit provides a full REST API for programmatic chain management.

docs.title: Starting the Server
docs.text:
fallbackrabbit serve --port 8000

# With SQLite persistence
fallbackrabbit serve --storage sqlite:///data/frabbit.db

# With API key authentication
FALLBACKRABBIT_API_KEYS="key1:admin,key2:read" fallbackrabbit serve

docs.title: Health
docs.text: Method Path Description GET /health Health check

docs.title: Chains
docs.text: Method Path Description POST /chains Create a chain GET /chains List all chains GET /chains/{id} Get a chain PATCH /chains/{id} Update a chain DELETE /chains/{id} Delete a chain GET /chains/{id}/routing Get routing info GET /chains/{id}/summary Get chain summary

docs.title: Testing
docs.text: Method Path Description POST /chains/{id}/test Run a test GET /test-results/{id} Get test results

docs.title: Export
docs.text: Method Path Description POST /chains/{id}/export Export chain config POST /export-template Export with custom template

docs.title: Dashboard
docs.text: Method Path Description GET /dashboard Dashboard UI GET /dashboard/ Dashboard UI (trailing slash)

docs.title: Create Chain
docs.text:
curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/chains \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "name": "production-chain",
    "providers": [
      {"name": "GPT-4", "model_id": "gpt-4", "priority": 0},
      {"name": "Claude", "model_id": "claude-3-sonnet", "priority": 1}
    ],
    "fallback_rules": [
      {"condition": "rate_limit", "action": "retry", "max_retries": 2},
      {"condition": "timeout", "action": "failover"}
    ]
  }'

docs.title: Run Test
docs.text:
curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/chains/{id}/test \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"prompt_count": 5}'

docs.title: Export
docs.text: curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/chains/{id}/export?format=litellm

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text: FallbackRabbit supports static API key authentication for the REST API and dashboard.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Set API keys via environment variable or server configuration:

# Simple: comma-separated keys
export FALLBACKRABBIT_API_KEYS="my-secret-key-1,my-secret-key-2"

# Labeled: name=key pairs
export FALLBACKRABBIT_API_KEYS="admin:sk-admin-123,reader:sk-reader-456"

Or programmatically:

from fallbackrabbit.server import create_app

app = create_app(
    storage_url="sqlite:///data/frabbit.db",
    api_keys={"admin": "sk-admin-123", "reader": "sk-reader-456"},
)

docs.title: X-API-Key Header
docs.text: curl -H "X-API-Key: sk-admin-123" http://localhost:8000/chains

docs.title: Bearer Token
docs.text: curl -H "Authorization: Bearer sk-admin-123" http://localhost:8000/chains

docs.title: Query Parameter
docs.text: curl http://localhost:8000/chains?api_key=sk-admin-123

docs.title: Skip Paths
docs.text:
The following paths skip authentication:

  • /health — Health check endpoint
  • /docs — OpenAPI documentation
  • /redoc — ReDoc documentation
  • /openapi.json — OpenAPI schema
  • /dashboard — Dashboard UI (browser access)

docs.title: Rate Limiting
docs.text:
API keys can be combined with rate limiting for production use:

fallbackrabbit serve \
  --storage sqlite:///data/frabbit.db \
  --rate-limit 100/minute

docs.title: Chain Configuration
docs.text: A chain defines the order of LLM providers and the rules for handling failures.

docs.title: Minimal Chain
docs.text:
name: my-chain
providers:
  - name: GPT-4
    model_id: gpt-4
    priority: 0

docs.title: Full Chain
docs.text:
name: production-chain
description: "Primary production fallback chain"

providers:
  - name: GPT-4
    model_id: gpt-4
    priority: 0
    latency_ms: 800
    max_output_tokens: 4096
    cost_per_1k_tokens: 0.03
    metadata:
      team: platform
      environment: production

  - name: Claude
    model_id: claude-3-sonnet
    priority: 1
    latency_ms: 600
    max_output_tokens: 4096
    cost_per_1k_tokens: 0.015
    metadata:
      team: platform

  - name: Gemini
    model_id: gemini-pro
    priority: 2
    latency_ms: 500
    max_output_tokens: 2048

fallback_rules:
  - condition: rate_limit
    action: retry
    max_retries: 2
    wait_ms: 1000
  - condition: timeout
    action: failover
  - condition: server_error
    action: failover
  - condition: auth_error
    action: fail
  - condition: connection_error
    action: failover
    max_retries: 1

test_prompts:
  - "Explain quantum computing in simple terms"
  - "Write a haiku about fallbacks"
  - "What is the capital of France?"

docs.title: Provider Fields
docs.text: Field Type Required Description name string ✅ Display name model_id string ✅ Model identifier (e.g., gpt-4, claude-3-sonnet) priority int ✅ Lower = higher priority (0 = primary) latency_ms int ❌ Simulated latency in milliseconds max_output_tokens int ❌ Maximum output tokens cost_per_1k_tokens float ❌ Cost per 1K tokens (USD) metadata dict ❌ Arbitrary key-value metadata

docs.title: Priority Ordering
docs.text: Providers are tried in priority order (0 first). When a provider fails and the fallback rule says failover, the next provider in priority order is used.

docs.title: Importing Existing Chains
docs.text:
You can import chains via the REST API or dashboard:

# From a YAML file
curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/chains \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d @my-chain.yaml

# From JSON
curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/chains \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name": "...", "providers": [...]}'

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Core: Chain builder, simulator, config export (LiteLLM, OpenRouter, LangChain, Haystack, custom)
  • CLI: init, validate, test, export, serve, analyze commands
  • REST API: 15+ endpoints for chain CRUD, testing, and export
  • Dashboard: Dark-themed SPA with chain CRUD, test runner, export, WebSocket live progress
  • WebSocket: Real-time test progress broadcasting per chain
  • Auth: Static API key authentication (header, Bearer, query param)
  • Rate Limiting: Token bucket with per-IP, global, and burst controls
  • Storage: In-memory and SQLite backends with thread-safe operations
  • Template Export: Jinja2 templates with built-in Terraform, Docker, K8s, and .env formats
  • Real Provider Calls: AsyncProviderClient for OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure, Ollama, custom endpoints
  • Rich CLI: Tables, panels, progress bars for all commands
  • Testing: 556 tests across all modules
  • CI: GitHub Actions with ruff, mypy, pytest-cov on Python 3.11 + 3.12

docs.title: fallbackrabbit init <path>
docs.text:
Generate a starter chain config file.

fallbackrabbit init my-chain.yaml

docs.title: fallbackrabbit validate <path>
docs.text:
Validate a chain config file.

fallbackrabbit validate my-chain.yaml

Options: - --strict — Treat warnings as errors

docs.title: fallbackrabbit test <path>
docs.text:
Run a simulation test on a chain config.

fallbackrabbit test my-chain.yaml

Options: - --prompts N — Number of test prompts (default: 5) - --outages <path> — Outage scenario file - --verbose — Show detailed per-prompt results

docs.title: fallbackrabbit export <path>
docs.text:
Export a chain config to various formats.

fallbackrabbit export my-chain.yaml --format litellm

Options: - --format FORMAT — Export format: litellm, openrouter, langchain, haystack, custom, template - --output PATH — Write to file instead of stdout - --template PATH — Jinja2 template file (for template format) - --extra-vars KEY=VALUE — Extra template variables (repeatable)

docs.title: fallbackrabbit serve
docs.text:
Start the REST API server and dashboard.

fallbackrabbit serve

Options: - --host HOST — Bind address (default: 127.0.0.1) - --port PORT — Port number (default: 8000) - --storage URL — Storage backend URL (default: memory://, or sqlite:///path.db) - --reload — Enable auto-reload for development

docs.title: fallbackrabbit analyze <path>
docs.text:
Analyze a chain config for issues and optimization opportunities.

fallbackrabbit analyze my-chain.yaml

docs.title: fallbackrabbit --version
docs.text: Show the installed version.

docs.title: fallbackrabbit serve
docs.text: Flag Default Description --host 127.0.0.1 Bind address --port 8000 Port number --storage memory:// Storage URL (memory:// or sqlite:///path.db) --reload false Enable auto-reload for development --api-keys — API keys (comma-separated or label:key pairs) --rate-limit 60 Per-IP rate limit per minute --rate-limit-global 600 Global rate limit per minute --rate-limit-burst 10 Burst allowance

docs.title: fallbackrabbit test
docs.text: Flag Default Description --prompts 5 Number of test prompts --outages — Outage scenario file --verbose false Detailed per-prompt results --real false Make real provider calls

docs.title: fallbackrabbit export
docs.text: Flag Default Description --format litellm Export format --output stdout Output file path --template — Jinja2 template file --extra-vars — Template variables (key=value, repeatable)

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: Variable Description OPENAI_API_KEY OpenAI API key ANTHROPIC_API_KEY Anthropic API key AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY Azure OpenAI API key AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT Azure OpenAI endpoint URL OLLAMA_BASE_URL Ollama base URL (default: http://localhost:11434) FALLBACKRABBIT_API_KEYS API keys for authentication FALLBACKRABBIT_STORAGE_URL Default storage URL

docs.title: Export Formats
docs.text: Format Description litellm LiteLLM model config openrouter OpenRouter routing config langchain LangChain router config haystack Haystack pipeline config custom Raw JSON dump template Jinja2 template-based

docs.title: Error Types
docs.text: Type HTTP Codes Default Action rate_limit 429 Retry timeout 408, 504 Failover server_error 500, 502, 503 Failover auth_error 401, 403 Fail connection_error — Failover validation_error 400 Fail

docs.title: Fallback Actions
docs.text: Action Behavior retry Retry same provider (up to max_retries) failover Try next provider wait Wait wait_ms then retry same provider fail Stop immediately

docs.title: Web Dashboard
docs.text: FallbackRabbit includes a dark-themed web dashboard for managing chains, running tests, and exporting configs.

docs.title: Access
docs.text:
fallbackrabbit serve
# Open http://localhost:8000/dashboard

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
• Chain statistics (total chains, providers, rules)
  • Quick actions (create chain, run test)

docs.title: Chains
docs.text:
• Create, view, edit, and delete chains
  • Provider list with priority ordering
  • Fallback rules configuration
  • Chain validation status

docs.title: Test Runner
docs.text:
• Select a chain and number of prompts
  • Run tests with live progress via WebSocket
  • View results: success rate, latency, provider usage
  • Outage scenario testing

docs.title: Export
docs.text:
• Export chains to any supported format
  • Built-in: LiteLLM, OpenRouter, LangChain, Haystack, custom
  • Template-based: Terraform, Docker, K8s, .env
  • Copy or download exported configs

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Dark theme — Easy on the eyes, matches the FallbackRabbit brand
  • Responsive — Works on mobile and desktop
  • WebSocket — Live test progress updates
  • Zero dependencies — No external CSS/JS required
  • API-powered — All data comes from the REST API

docs.title: Export Formats
docs.text: Export your chain configuration to various framework-specific formats.

docs.title: CLI Export
docs.text:
# LiteLLM config
fallbackrabbit export my-chain.yaml --format litellm

# OpenRouter config
fallbackrabbit export my-chain.yaml --format openrouter

# LangChain router config
fallbackrabbit export my-chain.yaml --format langchain

# Haystack pipeline config
fallbackrabbit export my-chain.yaml --format haystack

# Custom format
fallbackrabbit export my-chain.yaml --format custom

# Write to file
fallbackrabbit export my-chain.yaml --format litellm --output litellm_config.yaml

docs.title: API Export
docs.text: curl http://localhost:8000/chains/{id}/export?format=litellm

docs.title: LiteLLM
docs.text:
Generates a LiteLLM-compatible config with model list and fallbacks:

model_list:
  - model_name: production-chain
    litellm_params:
      model: gpt-4
  - model_name: production-chain
    litellm_params:
      model: claude-3-sonnet
  - model_name: production-chain
    litellm_params:
      model: gemini-pro
fallbacks:
  - production-chain

docs.title: OpenRouter
docs.text:
Generates an OpenRouter routing config:

{
  "models": {
    "primary": "gpt-4",
    "fallbacks": ["claude-3-sonnet", "gemini-pro"]
  }
}

docs.title: LangChain
docs.text:
Generates a LangChain router config:

{
  "model_router": {
    "models": [
      {"name": "GPT-4", "model": "gpt-4", "priority": 0},
      {"name": "Claude", "model": "claude-3-sonnet", "priority": 1}
    ],
    "default_fallback_action": "failover"
  }
}

docs.title: Haystack
docs.text:
Generates a Haystack pipeline config:

components:
  gpt_4:
    type: OpenAIGenerator
    params:
      model: gpt-4
  claude:
    type: OpenAIGenerator
    params:
      model: claude-3-sonnet
pipelines:
  - name: production-chain
    nodes:
      - name: gpt_4
        inputs: [Query]
      - name: claude
        inputs: [gpt_4]

docs.title: Custom Templates
docs.text:
Use Jinja2 templates for any format:

fallbackrabbit export my-chain.yaml --format template --template terraform.j2
fallbackrabbit export my-chain.yaml --format template --template docker-compose.j2 --extra-vars env=prod

Built-in templates: - terraform — Terraform provider configuration - docker — Docker Compose with environment variables - k8s — Kubernetes ConfigMap - env — .env file

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.11+
  • pip or uv

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
# From GitHub (private repo)
pip install git+https://github.com/MelaBuilt-AI/FallbackRabbit.git

# Or clone and install locally
git clone https://github.com/MelaBuilt-AI/FallbackRabbit.git
cd FallbackRabbit
pip install -e .

# With dev dependencies
pip install -e ".[dev]"

docs.title: Verify
docs.text:
fallbackrabbit --version
fallbackrabbit --help

docs.title: Running the Server
docs.text:
# Start with in-memory storage (default)
fallbackrabbit serve

# Start with SQLite persistence
fallbackrabbit serve --storage sqlite:///data/frabbit.db

# Custom host/port
fallbackrabbit serve --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8080

docs.title: Running Tests
docs.text:
# Full test suite
pytest

# With coverage
pytest --cov=fallbackrabbit --cov-report=html

# Specific test file
pytest tests/test_simulator.py -v

docs.title: Real Provider Calls
docs.text: FallbackRabbit can make real API calls to LLM providers for integration testing.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
Set environment variables for each provider you want to use:

export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="sk-ant-..."
export AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY="..."
export AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT="https://xxx.openai.azure.com"
export OLLAMA_BASE_URL="http://localhost:11434"

docs.title: Running Real Tests
docs.text:
# Enable real provider calls
fallbackrabbit test my-chain.yaml --real

# With outage simulation
fallbackrabbit test my-chain.yaml --real --outages outage-scenario.yaml

docs.title: Provider Configuration
docs.text:
Each provider supports custom configuration:

from fallbackrabbit.providers import AsyncProviderClient, ProviderConfig

config = ProviderConfig(
    provider="openai",
    model_id="gpt-4",
    api_key="sk-...",
    max_tokens=1000,
    temperature=0.7,
)

client = AsyncProviderClient(config)
result = await client.complete("Hello, world!")

docs.title: Supported Providers
docs.text: Provider Environment Variable Base URL OpenAI OPENAI_API_KEY https://api.openai.com/v1 Anthropic ANTHROPIC_API_KEY https://api.anthropic.com/v1 Azure OpenAI AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY Custom endpoint Ollama — http://localhost:11434 Custom — Custom endpoint

docs.title: Cost Tracking
docs.text:
Real calls track token usage and estimated cost per prompt in the chain report.

API Costs

Real provider calls consume tokens and incur costs. Use --prompts 1 for quick verification, and reserve larger batch tests for staging environments.

docs.title: 1. Create a Chain
docs.text:
fallbackrabbit init my-chain.yaml

This generates a starter chain config:

name: my-chain
providers:
  - name: GPT-4
    model_id: gpt-4
    priority: 0
    latency_ms: 800
  - name: Claude
    model_id: claude-3-sonnet
    priority: 1
    latency_ms: 600
  - name: Gemini
    model_id: gemini-pro
    priority: 2
    latency_ms: 500

fallback_rules:
  - condition: rate_limit
    action: retry
    max_retries: 2
    wait_ms: 1000
  - condition: timeout
    action: failover
  - condition: server_error
    action: failover
  - condition: auth_error
    action: fail

docs.title: 2. Validate
docs.text: fallbackrabbit validate my-chain.yaml

docs.title: 3. Test with Simulated Outages
docs.text:
# Basic test — all providers healthy
fallbackrabbit test my-chain.yaml

# Test with outages
fallbackrabbit test my-chain.yaml --outages outage-scenario.yaml

# Custom prompt count
fallbackrabbit test my-chain.yaml --prompts 20

docs.title: 4. Export
docs.text:
# Export to LiteLLM config
fallbackrabbit export my-chain.yaml --format litellm

# Export to LangChain router config
fallbackrabbit export my-chain.yaml --format langchain

# Export to custom template
fallbackrabbit export my-chain.yaml --format template --template terraform.j2

# All formats: litellm, openrouter, langchain, haystack, custom

docs.title: 5. Dashboard
docs.text:
fallbackrabbit serve
# Open http://localhost:8000/dashboard

The dashboard provides a full UI for creating chains, running tests with live progress, and exporting configs.

docs.title: 6. REST API
docs.text:
All functionality is available via the REST API:

# Create a chain
curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/chains \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name": "my-chain", "providers": [...], "fallback_rules": [...]}'

# List chains
curl http://localhost:8000/chains

# Run a test
curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/chains/{id}/test \
  -d '{"prompt_count": 5}'

# Export
curl http://localhost:8000/chains/{id}/export?format=litellm

docs.title: Rate Limiting
docs.text: Built-in token bucket rate limiting for the REST API.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
from fallbackrabbit.server import create_app

app = create_app(
    storage_url="memory://",
    rate_limit_per_minute=60,    # Per-IP rate limit
    rate_limit_global=600,        # Global rate limit
    rate_limit_burst=10,         # Burst allowance
)

docs.title: Default Limits
docs.text: Scope Default Description Per-IP 60/min Requests per IP per minute Global 600/min Total requests across all IPs Burst 10 Extra requests allowed in burst

docs.title: Response Headers
docs.text:
Rate-limited responses include standard headers:

X-RateLimit-Limit: 60
X-RateLimit-Remaining: 45
X-RateLimit-Reset: 1705312800
Retry-After: 30

docs.title: Skip Paths
docs.text:
These paths skip rate limiting:

  • /health
  • /docs
  • /openapi.json
  • /dashboard

docs.title: 429 Response
docs.text:
When rate limit is exceeded:

{
  "detail": "Rate limit exceeded",
  "retry_after": 30
}

docs.title: Production Settings
docs.text:
For production deployments, consider:

  • Lower per-IP limits (30/min) with higher burst (5)
  • Higher global limit (1000/min) for multi-user scenarios
  • Combine with API key authentication for per-key limits

docs.title: Fallback Rules
docs.text: Fallback rules define how the chain responds to different error types.

docs.title: Rule Structure
docs.text:
fallback_rules:
  - condition: rate_limit
    action: retry
    max_retries: 2
    wait_ms: 1000
  - condition: timeout
    action: failover
  - condition: auth_error
    action: fail

docs.title: Conditions (Error Types)
docs.text: Condition Description Default Status Codes rate_limit Provider rate-limited the request 429 timeout Request exceeded time limit 408, 504 server_error Provider returned 5xx 500, 502, 503 auth_error Authentication failed 401, 403 connection_error Network/connection failure — validation_error Request validation failed 400

docs.title: Actions
docs.text: Action Description retry Retry the same provider after wait_ms failover Try the next provider in priority order wait Wait wait_ms then retry the same provider fail Stop — don't try further providers

docs.title: retry
docs.text:
Retries the same provider up to max_retries times with wait_ms between attempts. After exhausting retries, applies the next matching rule or fails.

- condition: rate_limit
  action: retry
  max_retries: 2
  wait_ms: 1000

docs.title: failover
docs.text:
Immediately tries the next provider in the chain. No waiting, no retries on the current provider.

- condition: timeout
  action: failover

docs.title: wait
docs.text:
Pauses for wait_ms then retries the same provider. Useful for temporary outages.

- condition: server_error
  action: wait
  wait_ms: 5000

docs.title: fail
docs.text:
Stops the chain immediately. No further providers are tried.

- condition: auth_error
  action: fail

docs.title: Status Code Matching
docs.text:
Rules can specify exact HTTP status codes for more granular control:

- condition: server_error
  status_codes: [502, 503]
  action: failover
- condition: server_error
  status_codes: [500]
  action: retry
  max_retries: 1

More specific status code rules take priority over broader condition matches.

docs.title: Rule Priority
docs.text:
1. Exact status code match (e.g., 429)
  2. Condition type match (e.g., rate_limit)
  3. Default behavior: failover

docs.title: Simulating Outages
docs.text: Test how your chain handles failures before they happen in production.

docs.title: Basic Simulation
docs.text:
fallbackrabbit test my-chain.yaml

This runs 5 test prompts with all providers healthy, showing latency and success rates.

docs.title: Outage Scenarios
docs.text:
Create an outage scenario file to inject specific failures:

# outage-scenario.yaml
outages:
  - provider: GPT-4
    error_type: rate_limit
    duration_prompts: 3
    status_code: 429

  - provider: Claude
    error_type: timeout
    duration_prompts: 2

  - provider: Gemini
    error_type: server_error
    duration_prompts: 5
    status_code: 503

Run with the scenario:

fallbackrabbit test my-chain.yaml --outages outage-scenario.yaml

docs.title: Outage Fields
docs.text: Field Type Required Description provider string ✅ Provider name to affect error_type string ✅ Error type to simulate duration_prompts int ✅ How many prompts the outage lasts status_code int ❌ Specific HTTP status code error_message string ❌ Custom error message

docs.title: Simulation Output
docs.text:
The simulator reports:

  • Per-prompt results — Which provider handled each prompt, latency, retries
  • Chain report — Success rate, average latency, total tokens, provider usage breakdown
  • Fallback path — Visual representation of which failovers occurred
  • Timing — Retries used, total wait time per prompt

docs.title: Verbose Mode
docs.text:
fallbackrabbit test my-chain.yaml --outages outage-scenario.yaml --verbose

Shows detailed per-prompt breakdown including error messages and retry history.

docs.title: Persistent Storage
docs.text: FallbackRabbit supports in-memory and SQLite storage backends.

docs.title: Memory Storage (Default)
docs.text:
Chains are stored in memory and lost on restart. Good for development and testing.

fallbackrabbit serve
# or explicitly
fallbackrabbit serve --storage memory://

from fallbackrabbit.server import create_app
app = create_app(storage_url="memory://")

docs.title: SQLite Storage
docs.text:
Chains persist across restarts. Good for production.

fallbackrabbit serve --storage sqlite:///data/frabbit.db

from fallbackrabbit.server import create_app
app = create_app(storage_url="sqlite:///data/frabbit.db")

docs.title: SQLite Features
docs.text:
• Thread-safe with WAL mode
  • Automatic table creation on startup
  • JSON serialization for chain data
  • No external database server required

docs.title: Storage Backends
docs.text:
Both backends implement the same StorageBackend interface:

from fallbackrabbit.storage import MemoryStorage, SqliteStorage

# Memory
storage = MemoryStorage()

# SQLite
storage = SqliteStorage("sqlite:///data/frabbit.db")

docs.title: Operations
docs.text: Method Description create(chain) Store a new chain get(chain_id) Retrieve a chain list() List all chains update(chain_id, updates) Update a chain delete(chain_id) Delete a chain clear() Remove all chains

docs.title: Migration
docs.text:
Switch storage backends without data loss:

  1. Export chains via API: GET /chains
  2. Start server with new storage
  3. Import chains via API: POST /chains

docs.title: Custom Templates
docs.text: Export chains to any format using Jinja2 templates.

docs.title: Built-in Templates
docs.text: FallbackRabbit ships with 4 built-in templates:

docs.title: Terraform
docs.text:
fallbackrabbit export my-chain.yaml --format template --template terraform

Generates Terraform provider configuration.

docs.title: Docker Compose
docs.text:
fallbackrabbit export my-chain.yaml --format template --template docker

Generates Docker Compose with environment variables.

docs.title: Kubernetes ConfigMap
docs.text:
fallbackrabbit export my-chain.yaml --format template --template k8s

Generates a Kubernetes ConfigMap.

docs.title: Environment File
docs.text:
fallbackrabbit export my-chain.yaml --format template --template env

Generates a .env file with provider configuration.

docs.title: Custom Templates
docs.text:
Write your own Jinja2 templates:

# my-template.j2
# Generated by FallbackRabbit
{% for provider in chain.providers %}
PROVIDER_{{ provider.name | upper }}={{ provider.model_id }}
PROVIDER_{{ provider.name | upper }}_PRIORITY={{ provider.priority }}
{% endfor %}

FALLBACK_CHAIN={{ chain.providers | map(attribute='name') | join(',') }}

fallbackrabbit export my-chain.yaml --format template --template my-template.j2

docs.title: API Template Export
docs.text:
# Built-in template
curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/export-template \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"chain_id": "abc123", "template_name": "terraform"}'

# Custom template file
curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/export-template \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"chain_id": "abc123", "template_content": "..."}'

# With extra variables
curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/export-template \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"chain_id": "abc123", "template_name": "terraform", "extra_vars": {"env": "prod"}}'

docs.title: Template Variables
docs.text:
All templates receive:

Variable Type Description chain Chain The full chain object with providers and rules chain.name str Chain name chain.providers list Provider list with name, model_id, priority chain.fallback_rules list Fallback rules extra_vars dict Any extra variables passed via CLI or API

docs.title: WebSocket Events
docs.text: Real-time test progress via WebSocket.

docs.title: Connecting
docs.text:
const ws = new WebSocket('ws://localhost:8000/ws/test/{chain_id}');
ws.onmessage = (event) => {
  const data = JSON.parse(event.data);
  console.log(data);
};

docs.title: test_start
docs.text:
Emitted when a test begins.

{
  "type": "test_start",
  "chain_id": "abc123",
  "prompt_count": 5,
  "timestamp": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
}

docs.title: prompt_start
docs.text:
Emitted when a prompt begins processing.

{
  "type": "prompt_start",
  "chain_id": "abc123",
  "prompt_index": 0,
  "prompt_text": "Hello, world!"
}

docs.title: prompt_result
docs.text:
Emitted when a prompt completes.

{
  "type": "prompt_result",
  "chain_id": "abc123",
  "prompt_index": 0,
  "provider_used": "GPT-4",
  "success": true,
  "latency_ms": 823,
  "retries_used": 0,
  "total_wait_ms": 0
}

docs.title: test_complete
docs.text:
Emitted when all prompts are done.

{
  "type": "test_complete",
  "chain_id": "abc123",
  "success_rate": 0.8,
  "avg_latency_ms": 750,
  "total_prompts": 5
}

docs.title: JavaScript Client
docs.text:
function runTest(chainId, promptCount = 5) {
  const ws = new WebSocket(`ws://localhost:8000/ws/test/${chainId}`);

  ws.onopen = () => {
    console.log('Connected to test stream');
  };

  ws.onmessage = (event) => {
    const data = JSON.parse(event.data);

    switch (data.type) {
      case 'test_start':
        console.log(`Test started: ${data.prompt_count} prompts`);
        break;
      case 'prompt_result':
        console.log(`Prompt ${data.prompt_index}: ${data.success ? '✓' : '✗'} via ${data.provider_used}`);
        break;
      case 'test_complete':
        console.log(`Test complete: ${data.success_rate * 100}% success rate`);
        ws.close();
        break;
    }
  };
}
